mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount-rate channel dominates and equity multiples expand. A downward policy-rate trend should therefore lift S&P 500 returns over the next month, especially after prior tightening has ended.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of periods with a 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a recession-like threshold signals deteriorating household income and earnings expectations, which should pressure equities lower over the next month as risk premia widen and profit forecasts are cut.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with UNRATE above 8.5 produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise together, the signal indicates strengthening consumer demand plus payroll support, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through better sales and earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive RSAFS and PAYEMS condition does not outperform either series alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 60% of jointly expanding episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO after a prior plateau indicates weakening industrial activity and margin pressure for cyclical firms, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive in a majority of periods with a 3-month decline in INDPRO, or if the signal flips sign across repeated slowdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in CPIAUCSL combined with FEDFUNDS below the inflation rate indicates negative real policy rates and an inflation surprise. That combination should be bearish for equities over the next 20 trading days because it tightens real financial conditions and raises discount-rate uncertainty.
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-above-policy-rate regime does not correspond to below-median 20-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition fails to outperform CPIAUCSL alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and tighter future consumption growth, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next month as cyclicals and financials reprice weaker activity.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average after 3-month HOUST drops above 8%, or if the hit rate is below 45% across triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth remains positive while UNRATE is stable or falling, labor-market resilience supports household income and spending, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-resilience condition does not generate above-baseline 60-day returns, or if positive returns occur less often than 55% of the time under the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarterly change in GDPC1 captures broad real-activity acceleration that should support corporate revenues and sentiment, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect fails when GDP growth is flat or negative.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDPC1 changes are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative growth episodes, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.
